channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to delay TikTok ban so he can weigh in after he takes office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has asked the Supreme Court to pause the potential TikTok ban from going into effect until his administration can pursue a “political resolution” to the issue.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Requiring federal workers to return to office will be hard: Comer
item.description: U.S. Rep. James Comer believes evaluating the federal workforce's efficiency is critical to eliminating federal waste.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia lawmakers can subpoena Fani Willis for information related to Trump case, court rules
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis can be subpoenaed by Georgia lawmakers for information related to her case against Trump, a judge ruled on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A 9th telecoms firm has been hit by a massive Chinese espionage campaign, the White House says
item.description: A top White House official says a ninth U.S. telecoms firm has been confirmed to have been hacked as part of a sprawling Chinese espionage campaign that gave officials in Beijing access to private texts and phone conversations of an unknown number of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russian government says it is willing to improve ties—but onus is on Trump to make first move
item.description: Retired Lt. Gen. Keith Kellogg told Fox News that both Russia and Ukraine are ready for the war to end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WV Gov. Jim Justice to delay US Senate swearing-in, averting brief bedlam in Charleston
item.description: West Virginia would have had four governors in ten days if Justice were to take his Senate seat on-time, so the governor announced he will serve out his term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former FBI and CIA chief urges senators to sink Patel, Gabbard
item.description: William H. Webster, former FBI and CIA director, wrote a letter to U.S. senators urging them to reject President-elect Trump's choice of Kash Patel and Tulsi Gabbard to lead the FBI and national intelligence, respectively.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most Americans blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death, poll finds
item.description: Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger.
item.dc:creator: Linley Sanders, Tom Murphy And Amelia Thomson-Deveaux, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New 2025 laws hit hot topics from AI in movies to rapid-fire guns
item.description: New laws taking effect with the new year will affect Hollywood actors, social media stars and chatty motorists.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lawmakers push to expand agencies' power to cope with drones
item.description: Lawmakers continue to discuss legislation that would expand the authority of local police agencies to deal with drones.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Americans are exhausted by political news. TV ratings and a new AP-NORC poll show they're tuning out
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As a Democrat who immersed himself in political news during the presidential campaign, Ziad Aunallah has much in common with many Americans since the election. He's tuned out. “People are mentally exhausted,” said Aunallah, 45, of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fate of Trump's Cabinet picks unclear as Republicans prepare to take power in Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The fate of President-elect Donald Trump’s Cabinet is still unclear after Republican senators spent much of December carefully dodging questions about Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden takes heat for vetoing bill that would add new judgeships
item.description: Federal judiciary leadership and some lawmakers are criticizing President Biden for vetoing a bill that would’ve added 66 new federal judgeships amid growing caseloads.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump vows to direct DOJ to pursue death penalty in wake of Biden clemencies
item.description: President-elect Trump vowed Tuesday to direct his Department of Justice (DOJ) to pursue the death penalty in the wake of President Biden’s clemencies of nearly every federal death row inmate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden will decide on US Steel acquisition after influential panel fails to reach consensus
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A powerful government panel on Monday failed to reach consensus on the possible national security risks of a nearly $15 billion proposed deal for Nippon Steel of Japan to purchase U.S. Steel, leaving the decision to President Joe Biden, who opposes the deal.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, MARC LEVY and ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden vetoes once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal judgeships, citing 'hurried' House action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday vetoed a once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal district judgeships, saying “hurried action” by the House left important questions unanswered about the life-tenured positions.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden signs defense bill despite objections to ban on transgender health care for military children
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday signed into law a defense bill that authorizes significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members, aims to counter China's growing power and boosts overall military spending to $895 billion despite his objections to language stripping coverage
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules and state and federal laws.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Bill Clinton hospitalized with fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted Monday to Georgetown University Medical Center in Washington after developing a fever.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden’s death row commutations draws chorus of mixed reactions
item.description: A chorus of supporters and critics have emerged following the commutation of nearly every federal death row inmate by President Biden, whose decision came at a time when he’s trying to frame a so-far troubled legacy.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: House Ethics Committee accuses Gaetz of paying for sex, including with 17-year-old girl
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House Ethics Committee on Monday accused Matt Gaetz of “regularly” paying for sex, including once with a 17-year-old girl, and purchasing and using illicit drugs as a member of Congress, as lawmakers released the conclusions of a nearly four-year investigation that helped sink
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gaetz Ethics report finds ‘substantial evidence’ of statutory rape, drug use
item.description: The House Ethics Committee found “substantial evidence” that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid a 17-year-old high school student for sex when he was 35, used illicit drugs like cocaine and ecstasy, and obstructed Congress’s investigation into his conduct.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Panama will lose control of canal if it continues to 'rip off' US
item.description: President-elect Trump took issue with fees charged to use the Panama Canal, a vital shipping route, on Saturday night, claiming that he will demand oversight of the waterway be given back to the U.S. when he returns to office if it's not changed.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens to try to take back the Panama Canal
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Donald Trump suggested Sunday that his new administration could try to regain control of the Panama Canal that the United States “foolishly” ceded to its Central American ally, contending that shippers are charged “ridiculous” fees to pass through the vital transportation channel link
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes sentences of 37 federal death row prisoners
item.description: Opponents of the death penalty had urged President Joe Biden to take this step, given the number of executions that took place during President-elect Donald Trump's first term.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Broken tie in Minnesota House would give GOP muscle at start of 2025 session
item.description: Even with a slight majority, Republicans could flex newfound power at the start of the legislative session if DFL lawmaker’s disqualification stands.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms 235th Biden judge, surpassing Trump’s record
item.description: The Senate confirmed 234 of Trump’s judicial nominees during his first four years in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez faces uncertain political future
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) is facing an uncertain future after a pivotal setback this week, when she lost her race to lead Democrats on the House Oversight and Accountability Committee. The 74-year-old Rep. Gerry Connolly’s (D-Va.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: NJ rep. says it's alarming Feds can't bring down one drone
item.description: Chris Smith is disappointed by what he sees as a lack of transparency by the Biden administration over the New Jersey drone flap.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Government handling of drones a 'catastrophe': Elizondo
item.description: The FAA temporarily banned drone flights in areas of New Jersey and New York, where critical infrastructure is located.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the 34 Republicans that voted against the funding bill to avoid shutdown
item.description: More than 30 House Republicans voted against the government funding bill to avert a shutdown on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC
